title: Tween easings + agent-driven render debug overrides + positional audio

stories.title: Tween component gains a named easing curve
stories.summary: engine/core/systems/tween-system.ts grew a `easingCurves: Record<TweenEase, (t)=>number>` pure helper table keyed by name. v1 set: linear, easeInQuad / Cubic / Quart, easeOutQuad / Cubic / Quart, easeInOutQuad / Cubic / Quart, easeOutBack (small overshoot ~10%), easeOutElastic (damped oscillation past 1.0), plus the existing pulse (sine half-wave). Legacy aliases easeIn / easeOut / easeInOut remain and behave as the Quad variants for backwards-compat with existing scenes. easeFn() now reads the table; unknown names fall through to linear (no diagnostic needed — TS enum + default catch it).
verification: acceptance: A new Tween with `ease: "easeOutBack"` visibly overshoots its target value before settling (sample easeOutBack(0.5..0.9) returns >1.0 for some t).
engine/core/systems/tween-system.ts exports easingCurves table; TweenEase type widened from 5 to 16 names

+34 unit tests under tests/unit/tween-easing.test.ts: curve(0)=0, curve(1)=1 per curve; non-linear deviation at off-center sample for monotonic family; characteristic overshoot for easeOutBack + easeOutElastic; pulse 0→1→0 endpoints; Quad < Cubic < Quart slowdown at t=0.25; legacy aliases match Quad variants

stories.title: Agent-driven render debug overrides — wireframe / unlit-white / normals / uv
stories.summary: Add a renderer-level debug override toggle that an agent can flip at runtime. Surface: `runtime.renderer.setDebugMode(mode: 'off' | 'wireframe' | 'unlit-white' | 'normals' | 'uv')`. Adapter swaps every Mesh.material to a debug override material on flip (cached per-mesh so the original material is restored on 'off'). Dev-bridge: `POST /__agf/render/debug-mode { mode }` + `GET /__agf/render/debug-mode`. Useful tools: wireframe surfaces over-tessellation, unlit-white debugs shape vs lighting separately, normals catches flipped triangles, uv catches packed atlas bugs.
verification: Modes: off (default), wireframe (material.wireframe = true), unlit-white (replace with MeshBasicMaterial #ffffff), normals (custom ShaderMaterial → rgb = (n + 1) / 2), uv (custom ShaderMaterial → rgb = (uv.xy, 0))
setDebugMode('off') restores every original material; no leak in renderer.info().handleLeak after a full cycle
Dev-bridge route gets a row in docs/agent-probes.md
+ unit test on a stub adapter exercising the swap/restore lifecycle
+ Playwright smoke that flips wireframe via POST and checks renderer.info() still reports same draw count

stories.title: HUD pill shows current debug mode + frame counter pressure
stories.summary: Companion to AGF-RENDER-DEBUG-MODE-AGENT. When debug mode != 'off', surface a topRight HUD pill labelled `DEBUG: wireframe` (or whichever mode) so the player / agent can't accidentally leave the override on. Pill clears when mode flips back to 'off'.
verification: When runtime.renderer.setDebugMode('wireframe') is called, the HUD pill is mounted
Pill uses the existing HUD primitive (no new layer)

stories.title: Positional / spatial audio via Web Audio PannerNode
stories.deferredReason: First iteration ships project-local in Kaboom Crew (see KABOOM-AUDIO-POSITIONAL-ADOPT) — engine AudioBus is HTMLAudio-based and cannot panel-route, while Kaboom's audio-fx already owns a Web Audio AudioContext for procedural synth. Promote to engine when a second project needs spatial audio; the Kaboom implementation (PannerNode HRTF + listener wiring) will be the reference.
stories.summary: Engine audio currently routes every play() through the destination directly — fine for UI sounds, wrong for in-world events. Add an opt-in positional path: AudioBus.play accepts an optional `position: [x, y, z]` and a `listenerPosition` cached on the bus. When position is set, the synth chain ends at a PannerNode (HRTF / equal-power per option) instead of destination; the listener position is set from the active Camera each frame. Kaboom Crew picks this up for footstep + bomb-place + blast + death (each event's caller passes the bomber's world position).

stories.title: Kaboom Crew wires positional audio for in-world events
stories.summary: examples/kaboom-crew/src/audio-fx.ts grew an HRTF PannerNode path. `play(kind, context?)` takes an optional `PositionalPlayContext { position?: [x, y, z] }`; when supplied, the gain chain ends at a PannerNode (panningModel:HRTF, distanceModel:inverse, refDistance:2, rolloff:1, maxDistance:60) instead of context.destination. A new `setListenerPosition(x, y, z)` proxies to AudioListener (modern positionX/Y/Z AudioParam if available, else legacy setPosition). audio-binding-system resolves a `cellPos(entityId)` from GridPosition and forwards it on bomb-place / death / footstep / blast (pickup omits position — the entity is already despawned). bootstrap.ts pulls the player.1 cell from the status snapshot every fixedUpdate and calls audioFx.setListenerPosition(gx, 0, gz).
verification: acceptance: In Kaboom Crew, footsteps from a bomber positioned to the left of the camera pan audibly to the left ear and feel distance-attenuated when the bomber is far; match-win/-lose/-draw chimes still feel uniform/centered (they are intentionally non-positional UI chimes).
examples/kaboom-crew/src/audio-fx.ts: new PositionalPlayContext type + connectOutput helper that ends the chain in a PannerNode when position is set, otherwise direct-to-destination
examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/audio-binding-system.ts: cellPos(id) helper resolves [gx, 0, gz] from GridPosition; bomber-driven events forward it; match-* chimes don't
examples/kaboom-crew/bootstrap.ts: per fixedUpdate, reads playerSelf.{gx,gz} from status snapshot and calls audioFx.setListenerPosition
+4 unit tests in examples/kaboom-crew/tests/unit/audio-binding-system.test.ts: footstep / bomb-place carry [gx,0,gz]; blast carries BlastEvent origin; match-* chimes carry no position

+3 unit tests in examples/kaboom-crew/tests/unit/audio-fx.test.ts: play(kind, {position}) creates a PannerNode and routes gain → panner → destination; play() without position bypasses the panner; setListenerPosition forwards to AudioListener.setPosition
